questions.stem: Captain Kiwi wants to solve: A farmer has chickens and cows. He counts 10 heads and 28 legs. How many chickens does he have?

diagnostics.1: If 6 chickens (12 legs) + 4 cows (16 legs) = 28 legs. 6+4=10 heads.
diagnostics.2: 4 chickens would mean 6 cows = 4×2 + 6×4 = 32 legs, too many.
diagnostics.3: 5 chickens = 10 legs + 5 cows = 20 legs = 30, too many.

hint.level_3: Each time you swap a chicken for a rabbit, you gain 2 legs.
hint.level_4: Keep swapping until the leg count matches. How many swaps?
hint.level_5: Classic trick: start with all chickens, then swap to rabbits one at a time until legs match.

questions.stem: Ananya ate three-quarters of 4 mangoes. How many did she eat?
questions.visual_alt: Circle divided into equal parts showing fractions

diagnostics.1: Three-quarters = 3 out of 4 parts.
diagnostics.2: Half is bigger than quarter (2 parts vs 1 part).
diagnostics.3: Add the fractions: half + quarter = three-quarters.

hint.level_0: A fraction means equal parts of a whole.
hint.level_1: Half = 2 equal parts. Quarter = 4 equal parts.
hint.level_2: To find half, divide by 2. For quarter, divide by 4. For three-quarters, multiply quarter by 3.
